item.title: Why do politicians want AI to go faster?
item.description: Bullish rhetoric about ‘unleashing’ the technology is badly out of touch with the sensibilities of many voters

item.title: In the age of AI, the US must rethink the taxation of labour and capital
item.description: As the population ages and technology disrupts the economy, young workers are carrying a disproportionate tax burden

item.title: In trade’s ‘law of the jungle’, the winners are clear
item.description: It is China and the US that prosper when power sets the terms for global commerce

item.title: The City law firm that found wealth in private capital
item.description: Macfarlanes has transcended its roots to become one of London’s most profitable legal partnerships

item.title: When did democracy become an investment bubble?
item.description: A new closed-end fund is open to anyone with a brokerage account but whether it brings prosperity remains to be seen

item.title: Burford’s court loss shows risk in listed litigation funders
item.description: A volatile business often involving binary outcomes may not be suited for risk-averse public market investors

item.title: The missing middle in the defence of global trade
item.description: Talk of replacing the US as anchor of the multilateral system has come to little

item.title: Crypto has no place in UK political donations
item.description: Its inclusion will not extend democracy but reduce it

item.title: The new Obama museum promises to revitalise Chicago’s South Side
item.description: But reversing decades of disinvestment is not without its challenges

item.title: A blueprint for Chinese global leadership
item.description: With the US destroying its own credibility, the opportunity is Beijing’s for the taking

item.title: Iran could emerge from the war stronger and more dangerous
item.description: The Islamic republic aims to set up a toll booth on the Strait of Hormuz. It may succeed

item.title: Iran shocks make their way inexorably to the grocery aisle
item.description: Fertiliser is another casualty of the closed Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Short rates have overshot
item.description: Plus the social media lawsuits

item.title: What to do when the ‘public good’ of information goes bad
item.description: The creation and dissemination of reliable news is at an economic disadvantage

item.title: America’s gig economy
item.description: Self-employment, at both the high and the low end, is keeping consumption afloat — but for how long?

item.title: We said we didn’t want you working from home. Well it turns out we do
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: Should you change the name of your house?
item.description: The right one can add romance or gravitas, and tell a story — the wrong one can be naff, and scupper a sale

item.title: Investors need to rethink the ‘factor’ approach
item.description: Portfolio management should be based more on themes such as innovation

item.title: The Iran war will cement China’s superpower status
item.description: Beijing’s industrial prowess positions it for economic and diplomatic gains

item.title: Social media has been put on notice
item.description: Legal and legislative pressure is mounting to safeguard young people

item.title: Iran’s aggression will bring Gulf countries closer to the US
item.description: Despite their resentment, states in the region need American and Israeli technology to defend themselves

item.title: The Pentagon-Anthropic dispute is a test of control
item.description: Should private companies be able to set boundaries around the AI systems we integrate into our lives?

item.title: The hunger for ‘content’ is keeping us culturally stuck
item.description: The algorithms of the distraction economy are feeding us what we already like, creating a loop with no progress

item.title: In a world of $100 oil, fast fashion loses its defensive charms
item.description: Polyester-heavy retailers could soon start to feel the cost of their exposure to higher oil prices

item.title: The surprising science of quitting
item.description: Most of us are just one event away from resigning even though we probably should stay put
